channel.title: Comments for page "Hugo Hijinks"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Hugo Hijinks"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was it just me, or was anyone else able to completely hear Hugo's voice when he "talked". In his Matrix voice too, it was awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well this is entertaining as all hell.
There is a noticeable difference between the Aldon and Finn of kaktus and the Aldon and Finn of TwistedGears, but it really works for this particular tale.

+1.

item.title: Yay.
item.content:encoded:
I like this series, so it makes me feel warm and fuzzy inside to see it continue. Especially liked that Hugo Weavings was in this one. Oh joy.

Just to be certain, I'm not being sarcastic. I really did enjoy this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just love that the whole nature of this series is such that two broke artists can break into Hugo Weaving's house and ask him to play a role in every movie in a broken-down drive-in's stock, including some very minor and nonsensical ones(in exchange for nothing but a couple of admittedly cool golems).

And Hugo Weaving is just like, "Sure, whatever. Lemme get my coffee."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really charming, and while not my headcanon for SCP-1781… well fuck, it's practically canon now anyway. I'm sold.

item.title: insert-double-H-joke-here.png
item.content:encoded:
My deadline for this was about five months ago. Apologies to TwistedGears for the delay.

Anyway, this is Hugo Hijinks, the newest installment (and first interlude!) in the Learning the Alphabet series! Despite my incredibly poor ability to meet deadlines, this was a lot of fun to do and I am grateful for the opportunity to have done it.

Many thanks to everybody who looked at this in chat, specifically TwistedGears, Randomini, Decibelle, and a few others.
